sections.title: Searching the shelf with the diary in the inventory

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: Ah, hello again, what can I do for you now?

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: Ah, excellent, you've made your own serum and it seems to work well! Many thanks for using the Serum on me! But beware, I may start to change back soon!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: My name is Ulsquire Shauncy, I'm the local teacher and priest around here. You've really come at a time when Mort'ton is in a terrible state.

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: The emanations from the rest of Morytania affect the people here terribly turning them into mindless creatures. And, as if that wasn't enough, we get picked off by the evil Shades that exist in this area.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, you guys seem to be really at the bottom of the food chain around here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: This sad and afflicted little town is called 'Mort'ton'. In it's heyday it was a busy and bustling market town with a speciality for interring the dead.

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: The vile emanations from the Sanguinesti area started to spread and the town hasn't been the same since. The serum you made was the one that Herbi Flax was working on. Unfortunately the serum isn't permanent.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know how I could make the serum permanent?

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: I remember Herbi Flax talking about the serum. He said it worked on what he called the 'biological' effects of the affliction but that some other secondary effect was at work which he didn't quite understand.

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: He was interested in 'Flaemtaer', the ruined temple to the North. I found this odd. A man of science studying a structure dedicated to faith and the struggle of good verus evil, another concept he didn't understand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on around here?

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: The people are affected by the emanations from the rest of Morytania and the dead from the tombs have manifested into shadow creatures.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are all these shadowy creatures?

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: They are the disturbed spirits of those interred in the earth beneath Mort'ton. Their eternal spirits have been poisoned by the close vicinity to the evil nature of the inhabitants of the Sanguinesti area.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anyone else worth talking to around here?

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: Well, there's my good friend Razmire Keelgan. He's a prominent shop owner in Mort'ton, at least he was before he was affected by the area.

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: I know he tried to gather the militia together to fight the shades. He might know something about the temple. Razmire lives in the building to the west just over there, go and talk to him.

sections.title: Talking to him again before accepting to kill shades

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Ah, it's you, how's it going?

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Ah, it's you, how's it going?

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Hey, have you killed the five shades yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No not yet!

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Come back then when you have. And remember to bring me the remains, Ulsquire seems to think we might be able to send these poor souls to a better place.

sections.title: Talking to Ulsquire before giving Razmire 5 shade remains

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: Ah, hello again, what can I do for you now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How's it going? I talked to Razmire and he's asked me to slay five shades.

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: I can't say that I'm surprised, Razmire hates them, but it would be interesting to see the remains of one and investigate it somehow. I might find something useful!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Ah, excellent, you've made your own serum and it seems to work well! Many thanks for using the Serum on me! But beware, I may start to change back soon!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: My name is Razmire Keelgan, I run a couple of shops in Mort'ton but don't get much custom these days.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Which stores do you run?

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: I run a general store with a few useful items, and I have a building supplies store, I'm hoping that when things get better down here, people will want to buy items to repair their buildings.

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Well to be honest, all of my stock is packed away right now seeing as the town is all overrun with these Shades. I tell you what. You put an end to five Shades for me and I'll open the store for you!

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: I have details on that dilapidated temple to the North. These shades have a purpose - they jealously guard their burial treasure! If you can do me some favours, I could help you to locate it!

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Well to be honest, all of my stock is packed away right now seeing as the town is all overrun with these Shades. I tell you what. You put an end to five Shades for me and I'll open the store for you!

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: I have details on that dilapidated temple to the North. These shades have a purpose - they jealously guard their burial treasure! If you can do me some favours, I could help you to locate it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: This place is called Mort'ton, it used to be a quiet town with a specialism in death. That is to say that many burial tombs were created here in the naturally formed caves under this very village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on around here?

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Well, the people of the village, my usual customers... are all affected by the emanations from some place to the North East. Ulsquire can give you the name.

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Needless to say Herbi was working on a serum to help us and you stumbled across the recipe! I for one am grateful to you, but there's a lot left to do before Mort'ton is returned to it's former glory.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are all these shadowy creatures?

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Those disgusting entities are the filth that have ruined my life. They're the restless spirits of the dead who jealously guard their burial treasure yet feed on the life force of the living.

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: I've heard that they jealously guard their tomb treasure and that it's worth plundering. I need you to kill five shades and bring me their remains so that I can conduct some experiments. Will you do it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'll dispatch those dark and evil creatures.

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Great, that's what I wanted to hear. When you've done it, bring back all the remains, I want to inspect them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, not right now, I need to do something else first.

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Ok, fair enough...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything worth doing around here?

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Life is a daily struggle with those Shades around here. They attack when you least expect it...it's terrifying! I tell you what, if you kill five of those Shades I'll see if I can help you out! Is it a deal?

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Have you killed those Shades yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, sorry, not yet.

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Well, come back when you have.

sections.title: Returning without the five shade remains

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Ah, it's you, how's it going?

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Hey, have you killed the five shades yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I have actually!

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Hmm, yes, but you don't have the five shade remains I asked of you! Come back when you have them.

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Ah, it's you, how's it going?

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Hello again...what can I do for you now?

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: Ah, hello again, what can I do for you now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Razmire said to come and talk to you.

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: Oh yes, well, that's very nice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just slayed five shades for Razmire...he said that you might be interested in seeing the shade remains. Unfortunately I don't have any with me at the moment...

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: That is a shame, I would have liked to have seen the remains. Razmire was right. I'm keen to study them and perhaps see if I can work out a way to put their spirits to rest.

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Ah, it's you, how's it going?

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Hey, have you killed the five shades yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes I have actually!

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Great, I'll experiment on these! I think that Ulsquire might be keen to see some shade remains, if you take some over to him, he can possibly give you some extra information about them.

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Now that you've slayed some of those nasty shades, I feel motivated to help you out, I can trade with you now if you like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about the shades?

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Ulsqire says they're the incarnation of the dead who were interred in the tombs under Mort'ton. If you can find a way to put these spirits to rest, I'll show you a way into their lair!

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Mind you...there must be thousands of them buried under there! Just imagine all the treasure they must have buried under there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about that temple?

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: It's ancient and has been desecrated since before I was born. I've always felt that it was quite a nice place to sit and have a picnic, quite a nice view out of the swamps of Mort Myre.

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: I told Ulsquire that if he can afford to go half's with me on the building materials, I'd help him to try and fix it up one day. I've always wondered what it looked like back in pagan times.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know how I could make this serum permanent?

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: I'd say not my friend. That clever Flax chap was always experimentatin', I guess he was a scholarly type. He always dismissed Ulsquires learned speculations about the temple, I'd say that was a bit short sighted myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's that supposed to mean?

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Well, you made the serum yourself so I guess you'll experimentate with it. I'd say that the temple might be able to change the serum in some way. Well, yes, I guess it sounds superstitious, but what's to be lost?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you open a store for me?

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Sure, which store do you wanna see?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, thanks

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: Ah, hello again, what can I do for you now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Razmire said to come and talk to you.

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: Oh yes, well, that's very nice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just slayed 5 shades for Razmire, he said you might be interested in seeing some shade remains?

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: Oh yes, that would be interesting!

sections.title: Once the holy fire goes out
Attempting to repair the temple without the required materials
Returning to Ulsquire before making sacred oil

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: Ah, hello again, what can I do for you now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there, I've started repairing the temple.

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: That's great, carry on the good work. I'm sure that the temple is the key to unlocking a better life for all of us in Mort'ton.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do when the temple is built?

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: I've researched this much, it seems that the temple was dedicated to the worship of elemental flame or fire. The sacred flame was said to be very holy and was used somehow to sanctify oil for use in cremations.

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: The pagans believed that sacred oil sanctified the cremation ceremony and thus helped the deceased pass onto the next life, and it helped the logs to burn! In case there's any truth in the story, have this!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: The pagans believed that sacred oil sanctified the cremation ceremony and thus helped the deceased pass onto the next life, and it helped the logs to burn! You can buy olive oil from Razmire, it's quite cheap.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Shades keep knocking the temple down!

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: I'm not surprised. The temple represents holiness. They may feel that it has power over them in some way. My advice is to carry on and finish the temple if you can, and see what happens. You may need help though!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you mean?

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: You may need help building the temple or defending yourself against the Shades. I'm sure if you ask around nicely you would be able to get some help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about the Shades.

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: All undead hate mortal spirituality. It's a symbol of mortal hope in the face of death and the unknown. The temple represents the finality of true death where all ties to the mortal world are lost.

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: The Shades wish to forever possess the value of their tomb treasures and jealously guard them in death as they did in life. They are afraid of true death and so try to destroy anything which reminds them of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I keep running out of building materials!

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: I'd go and see Razmire, perhaps he has some items in stock?

sections.title: Returning to Razmire before making sacred oil

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Ah, it's you, how's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there, I've started repairing the temple.

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: That's great, carry on the good work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do when the temple is built?

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Well, you could always have a nice picnic... oh.. but then there's the problem with the Shades... hmmm, not nice... not nice at all! Well, I'll be honest, I don't really know! But I bet Ulsquire will know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Shades keep knocking the temple down!

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Interesting...perhaps they have something against the temple? Perhaps it represents something bad to them? Hmm, interesting stuff, but you'd best speak to Ulsquire about that sort of thing... do you need any supplies?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I keep running out of building materials!

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Oh really... that's interesting... I have just the thing for you!

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: Ah, hello again, what can I do for you now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you find out about the remains.

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: The shade remains are made of a strange substance which I've not come across before. I believe we should put these tormented spirits to rest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can we do that? You can't even bury the remains!

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: Quite right...I was thinking that a holy cremation would do the trick, but the funeral pyres need specially prepared 'pyre logs' - normal logs that have been treated with 'sacred oil' to give it a nice holy touch.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How would I make some sacred oil?

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: You won't! It's a pagan secret lost a long time ago. Normally I would try to bless some commonly found vegetable oil, but I'm so weak from this illness that I know I just won't be able to do it.

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: Huh! I used to joke with Herbi Flax that if 'Flaemtaer temple' was still standing, we could practice some ancient pagan rituals and incite the sacred flame! Huh, I'm not surprised that he never took me seriously!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can you tell me about that temple?

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: Hmm, interesting question. It is an ancient pagan temple referred to as Flaemtaer. I've studied it quite a lot, one day I hope to be able to rebuild it, who knows what ancient power it could unleash?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can you rebuild a totally destroyed temple?

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: I couldn't do it alone, I'd need a few expert artisans to help with the construction. I'd also need a good supply of limestone bricks, swamp paste and timber beams. Thankfully Razmire stocks all these items. (Sigh)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know how I could make this serum permanent?

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: The serum is a product of science. Herbi was a clever man to have made it, but he didn't integrate anything from the spiritual world into his work - and for all of his hard science there is still something missing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, you don't know then!

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: I'm a man of faith...that's all I know. Sometimes I feel that we should experiment with the sacred and the holy, not just the crude matter we find around us everyday.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do now?

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: That's up to you my friend! I once heard someone say that making your own decisions is the only real freedom you have. Exercise your freedom my friend, while you still can.

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: Ah, hello again, what can I do for you now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there, I used the olive oil on the flame and it gave me sacred oil! What should I do now?

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: From what I've learned, the ancients used to use the sacred oil on logs in order to make pyre logs for cremation. You could try that!

sections.title: Returning to Ulsquire after making pyre logs

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: Ah, hello again, what can I do for you now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there, I used the sacred oil on the logs and I made pyre logs! What should I do now?

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: Now you need to place the sacred logs on the pyre, then add the remains of a shade, then set fire to the pyre. This should release the Shade from it's eternal damnation and help it pass on to the other side.

sections.title: Putting the logs on the funeral pyre

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: Ah, hello again, what can I do for you now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've placed the logs on the pyre. What do I do now?

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: You need to place the remains of the shade on top of the logs and then set alight to it.

sections.title: Putting the remains on the funeral pyre
Returning to Razmire after burning shade remains

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Ah, it's you, how's it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, I've laid a Shade to rest, can you tell me where the Shade lair is now?

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Yeah, great job on putting that Shade to rest. The entrance to the Shade Lair is due North of my shop. Good hunting in the lair my friend!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you open a store for me?

lines.speaker: Razmire Keelgan
lines.text: Sure, which store do you wanna see?

sections.title: Talking to Ulsquire after burning shade remains

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: Ah, hello again, what can I do for you now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've put the Shade's spirit to rest!

lines.speaker: Ulsquire Shauncy
lines.text: Great! Well done my friend!
